About: It is about the Roman Republic and the emergence of the Roman Empire.
Affects: It affects the political conditions that led to the collapse of the Roman Republic.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the First Triumvirate, an informal political alliance.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the gens Julia, a patrician family.
BuiltIn: It was built across the river Rhine.
CreatedTo: It was created to replace the republican lunisolar calendar.
HasA: It has a lifetime seat in the Senate.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by military victories in the Gallic Wars.
Influenced: It influenced the emergence of the Roman Empire.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the political fortunes of the family.
IsA: It is a Roman general and statesman.
It is a dictator of the Roman Republic.
It is a member of the First Triumvirate.
It is a political alliance.
It is a historical figure.
It is a Roman territory.
It is a military commander.
It is an author and historian.
It is a praetor.
It is a priest of Jupiter.
It is a military tribune.
KnownAs: It is known as dictator perpetuo.
KnownFor: It is known for consolidating power.
It is known for proclaiming himself dictator for life.
It is known for leading Roman armies through the Gallic Wars.
It is known for defeating his political rival Pompey.
It is known for invading Britain.
It is known for building a bridge across the river Rhine.
It is known for assuming control of government.
It is known for a vigorous reform and building programme.
It is known for creating the Julian calendar.
It is known for extending citizenship to communities in Spain and northern Italy.
MemberOf: It is a member of the First Triumvirate.
It is a member of the aristocracy.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Second Punic War.
It occurred during the Third Mithridatic War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in 60 BC.
It occurred in 51 BC.
It occurred in 50 BC.
It occurred in 49 BC.
It occurred in 45 BC.
It occurred in 44 BC.
It occurred in 73 BC.
It occurred in 71 BC.
It occurred in 70 BC.
It occurred in 69 BC.
OccurredOn: It occurred on the Ides of March.
PartOf: It is part of the Roman Republic.
It is part of the Roman Empire.
PublishedAs: It was published as dictator perpetuo.
UsedAs: It is used as a synonym for emperor.
UsedFor: It is used for political alliance.
It is used for military campaigns.
UsedIn: It is used in the Roman Empire.
UsedTo: It is used to collect naval reinforcements.
It is used to collect troops.
It is used to extend citizenship.
It is used to reduce the size of the grain dole.
It is used to settle veterans in new overseas colonies.
It is used to increase the size of the Senate.
